title: Chollas

'''Chollas''' were herd animals that grazed the plains on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TDC" /> Around [[392 BBY/Legends|392 BBY]],<ref name="Settling">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' states that the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] was settled "four [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] ago." ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' to "eight years after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]," for a date of Kal'Shebbol's settling to be established as [[392 BBY/Legends|392 BBY]].</ref> [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] settlers, escaping from [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]], domesticated the herds that roamed their adopted [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Along with the development of [[Farm/Legends|farms]], the ranching of the chollas helped the Twi'leks to establish the basis of their new society.<ref name="TDC" /> By [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' to "eight years after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]," for a date of [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].</ref> chollas were also found on the neighboring planet of [[Kolatill]]. The ranching of both chollas and [[Bantha/Legends|banthas]] was a large industry on the planet; the hides and meat of the chollas sold for decent sums of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] in Kolatill's northern [[City/Legends|cities]]. Both the meat and hide were also exported off-world to the nearby planets of [[Gandle Ott]] and [[Torize]].<ref name="O">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Omens|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Chollas were first referenced in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]] in their information capsules for the planets Kal'Shebbol and Kolatill. It is unknown if ''chollas'' is the plural or singular name of the species. The species is referred to as ''herds of chollas''; however, this article makes no assumptions as to whether or not the name is singular or plural, and lists it by the only name indicated.<ref name="O" />
